untranslatable_reason: Pattern detection requires multi-bar analysis of price swing extremes and horizontal consolidation over months. The core pattern (repeated declining lows with failed recoveries, months of sideways movement) cannot be fully expressed with single-bar indicators. Donchian channels approximate extreme breakouts, but detecting the specific 'broadening' structure with alternating lows and failed resistance breaks requires pattern recognition logic not available in single-condition rules.
translation_notes: Approximated using Donchian channels for breakout detection above/below formation extremes. Entry conditions attempt to capture pivot-relative reversals. However, a true Broadening Top pattern requires detecting: (1) declining swing lows over time, (2) failed recovery attempts capped by prior resistance, and (3) extended horizontal consolidation—all of which exceed the scope of atomic indicator conditions. Stop-loss set to 3% as reasonable default (author silent); hold period set to 180 days to accommodate months of consolidation mentioned in pattern description.
